name: His Majesty the Worm
description: Guild Book content pack for His Majesty the Worm: kith & kin, the four paths and their talents, the full Omphalic Market, conditions, afflictions, languages, and dungeon denizens. Game text reproduced verbatim from the core rulebook under the Adherent of the Worm open licence.
license: Adherent of the Worm open licence (https://www.hismajestytheworm.games/open-license): "the mechanics and game text of His Majesty the Worm may be reused freely." His Majesty the Worm is copyright Joshua McCrowell. Game text reproduced verbatim; no book artwork, logos, or trade dress reproduced.

attributes.name: Swords
attributes.description: Swords represents strength and skill with weapons. Test Swords to bend the bars of your cell or tip over a heavy bookshelf onto an unsuspecting goblin.

attributes.name: Pentacles
attributes.description: Pentacles represents swiftness and slyness. Test Pentacles to sneak past a deathknight or slip the sea witch’s conch into your bag without her noticing.

attributes.name: Cups
attributes.description: Cups represents wisdom and training. Test Cups to activate an ancient clockwork mechanism or coax a wild winter wolf to accept a gift of meat from your hand.

attributes.name: Wands
attributes.description: Wands represents willpower and charisma. Test Wands to convince an art critic that your petrified companion is actually avant-garde art or overcome the dominating gaze of a vampire.

outcomes.label: Great success
outcomes.description:
If the total value is 14+ *and* the player drew the suit being tested on the initial draw (not from pushing fate or another source), the result is a great success. Not only does the adventurer succeed, but they succeed extremely well, going above and beyond what was expected.

The GM should invite the player to embellish how *impressive*, how *effortless*, how *quick*, or how *thorough* their success was.

outcomes.label: Success
outcomes.description: If the total value is 14+, the result is a success. The GM describes how the adventurer succeeds in their stated goal.

outcomes.label: Failure
outcomes.description: If the total value is 13 or less, the result is a failure. The GM describes how the adventurer fails and asks what they want to do next.

outcomes.label: Great failure
outcomes.description: If the player pushes fate *and* the total value is still 13 or less, the test is a great failure. The GM describes a *new* significant setback, bad situation, or harmful repercussion as a result. See “Pushing Fate.”

groupOutcomes.label: Tight spot
groupOutcomes.description: The group comes short of succeeding and are put in a tight spot.
